channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was expected to strengthen into a hurricane on Saturday as forecasters warned of massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding and landslides in the northern Caribbean, with an astounding 35 inches (89 centimeters) of rain anticipated in southwest Hai
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Just days after taking office, Japan's new leader faces a series of back-to-back foreign policy tests, with a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Tokyo sandwiched between Asia-region summits in Malaysia and South Korea.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? The mystery man in an AP photo after the Louvre jewel heist creates a buzz
item.description: PARIS (AP) — It was shortly after the stunning heist of the crown jewels at the Louvre when Paris-based Associated Press photographer Thibault Camus caught in his frame a dapperly dressed young man walking by uniformed French police officers, their car blocking one of the museum gates.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Security video captures moment former mayor stabbed multiple times outside his blue state business
item.description: Security video shows the moment a former Massachusetts mayor was stabbed on Monday afternoon outside his cannabis dispensary, leaving him hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials investigating after deadly crane collapse in commercial area north of Boston
item.description: Authorities, including officials from OSHA, are investigating after a fatal crane collapse at a commercial site north of Boston in Everett, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Alex Murdaugh's empire, BTK's family fallout, Bryan Kohberger's money woes
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Family of missing Kentucky teen asks for her safe return, offers cash reward
item.description: After more than a week of searching and distributing flyers, the family is wondering why more hasn't been done.
item.dc:creator: Daegiona Wilson, NewsNation

item.title: 4 family members of Illinois governor candidate killed in Montana helicopter crash, campaign says
item.description: Four family members of a Republican running for governor in Illinois were killed in a Montana helicopter crash, his campaign said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, KRON4

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Broadway musicians reach tentative labor deal, averting a strike
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The union representing Broadway's musicians reached a tentative labor agreement with commercial producers on Thursday, averting a potentially crippling strike that would have silenced nearly two dozen musicals.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of US attacks off South America and what Congress has had to say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In less than two months, President Donald Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth say the U.S. military has killed at least 37 people in nine strikes against drug-smuggling vessels in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic Sen. Merkley of Oregon speaks for more than 22 hours to protest Trump amid shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Led by Oregon Sen. Jeff Merkley, Democrats seized the Senate floor on Wednesday to protest President Donald Trump's presidency amid the government shutdown and push for Republicans to negotiate with them on expiring health subsidies.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New US citizenship test proving difficult for immigrants
item.description: A new citizenship test that was rolled out this week by the Trump administration is being criticized as too difficult and could deter people from becoming U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Couple accused of moving, leaving child behind while he was at school
item.description: A 12-year-old Texas boy came home from school to find it empty and his family gone, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota court gives win to transgender powerlifter who was barred from competing as a woman
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The Minnesota Supreme Court on Wednesday delivered a win to a transgender weightlifter, saying she's protected by the state's expansive human rights law, while the organization that banned her from competing in its women's division said it still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Spirit Christmas is expanding: 30 locations announced across 13 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR/WHTM) – It may seem like Spirit Halloween has only just taken over your nearest shuttered Rite Aid and Party City locations, but some may soon become Spirit Christmas locations within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: You didn't win: Mega Millions jackpot grows to $680 million
item.description: No one won big in Tuesday's drawing, and the Mega Millions jackpot has grown to an estimated $680 million.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: These are America’s safest – and most dangerous – cities, according to WalletHub
item.description: Threats ranging from public health and natural disasters to financial harm are among the many factors Americans face when they consider moving to a different city.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: A United Airlines emergency landing likely caused by collision with a weather balloon
item.description: A United Airlines plane appears to have collided with a weather balloon while cruising over Utah at 36,000 feet last week, fracturing layers of its windshield and forcing an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One of the world's rarest whales that makes the Atlantic its home grows in population
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — One of the rarest whales on the planet has continued an encouraging trend of population growth in the wake of new efforts to protect the giant animals, according to scientists who study them.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pilots mistakenly thought someone was trying to breach cockpit during American Airlines flight
item.description: A Los Angeles-bound flight made an emergency landing on Monday after its pilots mistakenly thought someone was trying to breach the cockpit when its flight attendants banged on the locked door because the plane’s communication system went down.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
